title: Pilgrim's Progress §96: Christian then seeing them lie in this case went to them, if peradventure he might awak...
body: Christian then seeing them lie in this case went to them, if peradventure he might awake them, and cried, You are like them that sleep on the top of a mast, for the Dead Sea is under you--a gulf that hath no bottom. Awake, therefore, and come away; be willing also, and I will help you off with your irons. He also told them, If he that "goeth about like a roaring lion" comes by, you will certainly become a prey to his teeth. With that they looked upon him, and began to reply in this sort: Simple said, "I see no danger"; Sloth said, "Yet a little more sleep"; and Presumption said, "Every fat must stand upon its own bottom; what is the answer else that I should give thee?" And so they lay down to sleep again, and Christian went on his way.
author: engine

witnesses.label: First edition Nathaniel Ponder, London 1678 — original imprint
Cited extensively by Spurgeon, Edwards, modern Reformed writers
